channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lab-grown insulin-producing cells restore blood sugar control in diabetic mice
item.description: Researchers in Sweden have developed an improved method for generating insulin-producing cells from human stem cells, marking a significant advance in the effort to treat type 1 diabetes. The study, c...

item.title: Montreal Canadiens Fall 4-2 to Buffalo in Series Opener
item.description: The Montreal Canadiens, the last remaining Canadian team in the playoffs, opened their second-round series against the Buffalo Sabres with a 4-2 loss. This matchup contrasts sharply with Montreal's pr...

item.title: DG ISPR reports rise in terrorist attacks following Marka-i-Haq
item.description: On the one-year anniversary of Marka-i-Haq, DG ISPR Lt Gen Ahmed Sharif Chaudhry held a press conference alongside senior naval and air staff. He outlined ten strategic consequences of the 2025 milita...

item.title: ARN shareholders overwhelmingly reject CEO pay package during legal dispute with former hosts
item.description: At ARN Media's recent annual meeting, investors delivered a significant rebuke by overwhelmingly voting down the $1.1 million salary package proposed for new CEO Michael Stephenson. The protest vote, ...

item.title: Sudbury's Kingsway site redeveloped for commercial use
item.description: The Kingsway property in Sudbury, previously vacant, is undergoing redevelopment and is set to reopen as a business park. Local authorities and developers are collaborating to transform the site into ...

item.title: ISPR highlights strategic outcomes on first anniversary of Marka-e-Haq conflict
item.description: Director General of Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR), Lieutenant General Ahmed Sharif Chaudhry, addressed a press conference marking the first anniversary of Marka-e-Haq, alongside senior naval ...

item.title: Ouaddou backs in-form Chaine for Bafana after record clean-sheet season
item.description: Orlando Pirates coach Abdeslam Ouaddou has thrown his full support behind goalkeeper Sipho Chaine, saying he deserves a place in the Bafana Bafana squad for the upcoming FIFA World Cup. Chaine has bee...

item.title: Karangu Muraya Supports Late Brother’s Daughter by Enrolling Her in Tertiary Education
item.description: Gospel singer and philanthropist Karangu Muraya has shown real family values by supporting his late brother Paul Muraya’s daughter, Alicia Nymo. After Paul’s passing, Alicia came into public view requ...

item.title: Highlights from the 2026 Met Gala Red Carpet and Celebrity Looks
item.description: The 2026 Met Gala celebrated 'costume art,' bringing together celebrities and designers for a night of extravagant fashion and creativity. The event was co-chaired by Nicole Kidman, Venus Williams, An...

item.title: Dela Rosa Submits Two Bills While Remaining Absent from Senate Sessions
item.description: Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa has filed two proposed bills even as he continues to be absent from Senate sessions since November 2025. Based on official Senate records, the measures were submitted o...

item.title: Research links falls in middle age to higher risk of developing dementia later in life
item.description: A large review of existing studies suggests that experiencing falls after the age of 40 may be linked to an increased risk of developing dementia later in life. Researchers from the Changchun Universi...

item.title: Johannesburg’s Financial Crisis: DA Candidate Highlights Severe Municipal Challenges
item.description: DA mayoral candidate Helen Zille has warned that Johannesburg is on the brink of financial collapse, following a stark letter from Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana to Mayor Dada Morero. According to ...

item.title: US judge orders release of unauthenticated note linked to Jeffrey Epstein’s 2019 jail incident
item.description: A United States judge has ordered the public release of a handwritten document described as a suicide note allegedly linked to Jeffrey Epstein, the disgraced financier who died in jail in 2019. The no...

item.title: Performance artist Narcissister explores identity, masks, and spectacle in Melbourne shows
item.description: US performance artist Narcissister is known for her provocative shows that combine nudity, theatricality, and masked anonymity. She will appear at Melbourne’s Rising Festival presenting both a large-s...

item.title: Davao City railway deal claim is false; image shows fictional anime character
item.description: A recent social media post claimed that Davao City Mayor Sebastian “Baste” Duterte signed a historic railway contract with a Japanese official, promising a subway system for the city. The post include...

item.title: Burglary suspect arrested in Oregon after vehicle crash during police pursuit
item.description: Authorities in Clackamas County, Oregon, arrested a burglary suspect after a police pursuit ended in a multi-vehicle crash. Deputies initially responded to a report on Southeast River Road where a hom...

item.title: Tropical Storm Hagupit Expected to Enter Philippine Area of Responsibility This Weekend
item.description: According to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA), Tropical Storm Hagupit is projected to enter the Philippine Area of Responsibility (PAR) between...

item.title: Local B.C. group explores bid to keep Vancouver Whitecaps amid relocation concerns
item.description: A British Columbia–based group is preparing a potential bid to purchase the Vancouver Whitecaps, according to B.C. Jobs Minister Ravi Kahlon. The development comes shortly after a U.S.-led group annou...

item.title: Brunson and Knicks edge out 76ers to take 2-0 Eastern Conference semi-final lead
item.description: Jalen Brunson scored 26 points and played a crucial role in leading the New York Knicks to a 108-102 victory over the Philadelphia 76ers, giving them a 2-0 lead in the Eastern Conference semi-finals. ...

item.title: Young man seriously injured in unprovoked park stabbing in Dalby
item.description: A 23-year-old man was seriously injured in what police describe as an unprovoked attack while walking through a park in Dalby, west of Brisbane. The incident occurred around 2:30pm when the victim was...
